modules.title: Cyber Crime
topics: Definition, nature and extent of cyber crimes in India and other countries
Differences between conventional crimes and cyber crimes
Trends in cyber crimes across the world

modules.title: Forms of Cyber Crimes
topics: Cyber bullying, hacking, cracking, DoS attacks
Viruses, worms, logical bombs, time bombs, email bombing
Data diddling, salami attacks, phishing, steganography, cyberstalking, spoofing, cyber pornography, defamation
Computer vandalism, crimes through social networking sites, malware, social engineering
Credit‑card frauds, financial frauds, telecom frauds
Cloud‑based frauds, e‑commerce frauds and other emerging forms

modules.title: Profile of Cyber Criminals
topics: Cyber crime psychology and related theoretical frameworks
Learning, motivation, personality and intelligence theories of cyber criminals
Economic impact of cyber crimes on individuals, corporations, governments and nations
Psychological and sociological impact of cyber crimes

modules.title: Modus Operandi of Various Cyber Crimes and Frauds
topics: Fraud triangle and fraud detection techniques with counter‑measures
Intrusion analysis as a core skill set
Methods for performing intrusion analysis and the intrusion kill chain
Passive discovery of activity in historical data and logs
Detecting future threat actions and capabilities
Denial of access to threats, delaying and degrading adversary tactics and malware
Identifying intrusion patterns and key indicators

reference_books: Thomus J. Halt, Adam M. Bossler and Kathryn C. Seigfried‑Spellar, *Cyber Crime Investigation & Digital Forensic: An Introduction*, 2nd edition.

modules.title: The Software Product and Software Process
topics: Software Process Models: Linear Sequential Model, Prototyping Model, RAD Model, Evolutionary Process Models such as Incremental Model, Spiral Model, Component Assembly Model, RUP and Agile processes

modules.title: Requirement Elicitation, Analysis, and Specification
topics: Analysis modeling for function‑oriented and object‑oriented software development

modules.title: Software Analysis and Testing
topics: White‑box unit testing and unit‑testing frameworks
System testing and other specialized testing
Introduction to object‑oriented analysis, design and comparison with structured software engineering

modules.title: Software Maintenance & Software Project Measurement
topics: Reengineering, reverse engineering and tool support
Software effort, schedule and cost estimations

experiments: Lab work: running case‑study project covering all phases of the software development life‑cycle – requirements modeling, architecture and detailed design, coding (using Rational Rose, Eclipse, NetBeans or Visual Studio), and testing.
reference_books: Pankaj Jalote, “An Integrated Approach to Software Engineering”, Narosa Publishers, 2005.
Rajib Mall, “Fundamentals of Software Engineering”, Second Edition, PHI Learning.
R. S. Pressman, “Software Engineering: A Practitioner’s Approach”, Sixth Edition, McGraw‑Hill, 2006.
Sommerville, “Software Engineering”, Pearson Education.
Richard H. Thayer, “Software Engineering & Project Management”, Wiley India.
Waman S. Jawadekar, “Software Engineering”, TMH.
Bob Hughes, M. Cotterell, Rajib Mall, “Software Project Management”, McGraw‑Hill.

modules.title: Introduction
topics: Web‑design issues: browser compatibility, bandwidth, cache
Look and feel of a website

modules.title: Style Sheets, JavaScript and DHTML
topics: JavaScript variables, functions, conditions, loops and repetition
Advanced JavaScript: objects and custom objects
Document Object Model (DOM) and browser environments
DHTML – combining HTML, CSS and JavaScript

modules.title: XML and PHP
topics: Document Type Definitions (DTD) and XML Schemas
Transforming XML with XSL and XSLT
Introduction to PHP and basic syntax
Decision structures and looping in PHP
Browser control and detection in PHP
Advanced PHP features: cookies and sessions

modules.title: PHP and MySQL
topics: Connecting to MySQL server from PHP

reference_books: Developing Web Applications, Ralph Moseley and M. T. Savaliya, Wiley‑India
Web Technologies, Black Book, Dreamtech Press
HTML 5, Black Book, Dreamtech Press
Web Design, Joel Sklar, Cengage Learning
Developing Web Applications in PHP and AJAX, Harwani, McGraw‑Hill
Internet and World Wide Web How to program, P. J. Deitel & H. M. Deitel, Pearson

modules.title: History and Classical Cryptography
topics: Classical cryptography: shift cipher, monoalphabetic substitution cipher, polyalphabetic substitution cipher
Encryption with perfect secrecy and one‑time pad
Implementation aspects: shared secret randomness vs perfect secrecy
Modern cryptography principles: one‑way functions, trapdoor functions, hard‑core bits
Construction of a public‑key cryptosystem based on general cryptographic primitives
Implementation aspects: computational efficiency vs hardness

modules.title: Number Theory, Reductions and Symmetric Encryption
topics: Algorithmic number theory and cryptographic assumptions
Number‑theoretic candidates for cryptographic primitives (e.g., factoring)
Public‑key cryptosystems derived from number‑theory problems
Large integer arithmetic for implemented public‑key cryptosystems
Randomness and pseudo‑randomness: PRGs, PRFs, permutations
Symmetric encryption: security notions and schemes based on pseudo‑randomness primitives
Security proofs for symmetric encryption schemes
Implementation aspects: generating and testing randomness

modules.title: Block Ciphers, Modes, Cryptanalysis and Message Authentication
topics: Modes of operation: ECB, CBC, OFB, Counter, GCM
Cryptanalysis attacks: exhaustive, linear, differential, meet‑in‑the‑middle
Key length considerations and security‑performance trade‑offs
Message authentication: CBC‑MAC, HMAC, collision‑resistant hashes (MD5, SHA‑1, SHA‑2, SHA‑3)
Application case study: password‑based secure computer access

modules.title: Asymmetric Encryption and Advanced Primitives
topics: Number‑theoretic primitives: discrete logarithms, elliptic curves
Asymmetric encryption constructions: RSA variants, El Gamal, hybrid encryption
Malleable and homomorphic encryption: Paillier, brief overview of Gentry’s scheme
Advanced schemes achieving various security notions (e.g., Cramer‑Shoup)

reference_books: J. Katz and Y. Lindell, Introduction to Modern Cryptography: Principles and Protocols, Chapman & Hall/CRC Press, 2nd edition
A. Menezes, P. Van Oorschot, S. Vanstone, Handbook of Applied Cryptography, CRC Press, August 2001

modules.title: Introduction to compiling & Lexical Analysis
topics: Compiler structure: analysis‑synthesis model of compilation
Design of a lexical analyzer generator

modules.title: Syntax Analysis & Syntax Directed Translation
topics: Syntax‑directed definitions: construction of syntax trees

modules.title: Type Checking & Run‑Time Environment
topics: Specification of a simple type checker

modules.title: Code Generation
topics: Code generation issues in the design of a code generator

modules.title: Code Optimization
topics: Sources of optimization for basic blocks
Data‑flow analysis of structured flow graphs
